required_inputs: request intent and requested admissions action
hospital admissions specialty boundary and adjacent specialist map
clinical, utilization review, payer, privacy, coding, revenue-cycle, discharge, and bed-control authority map
refusal_triggers: request asks for clinical admission decision, diagnosis, treatment planning, medical necessity, level-of-care judgment, or discharge readiness
request asks for payer authorization approval, payer policy interpretation, coding, claim correction, denial appeal, or financial approval
request asks to accept, reserve, admit, or place a patient despite unresolved identity, order, eligibility, authorization, referral, placement, privacy, or tenant-policy blockers

required_inputs: public healthcare regulator, payer/provider operating, workflow, and patient-identification sources
tenant payer, plan, facility, service-line, bed-management, source-hierarchy, and jurisdiction overlays
source freshness, refresh interval, and decay metadata
required_outputs: source conflict or refresh escalation note
refusal_triggers: required public source or tenant source context is missing
public baseline, payer rule, tenant policy, facility rule, or jurisdictional rule conflicts materially
source freshness has decayed for a rule that controls admissions disposition

required_inputs: EHR, ADT, registration, order, bed-management, eligibility, payer portal, authorization, referral, utilization review, case management, transfer center, privacy, and audit records

patient/member identifiers, encounter class, admission request type, facility, service line, service date, admit source, attending practitioner, payer, plan, referral, authorization, and bed request context
audit, privacy, documentation, and admissions-readiness requirements
refusal_triggers: critical admission artifact is absent, stale, contradictory, unsigned, ambiguous, or not retrievable
traceability from prerequisite to source system and owner cannot be established
system evidence does not support the requested accepted, placed, or ready state

required_inputs: systems-of-record map for EHR, ADT, bed board, registration, order entry, eligibility, payer portal, authorization, referral, utilization review, transfer center, case management, privacy, and audit systems
dependency owners, routing matrix, tenant source hierarchy, and retrieval hooks
identity, encounter, order, eligibility, referral, authorization, bed/placement, consent, privacy, and documentation prerequisites
refusal_triggers: critical dependency owner is unresolved
systems of record materially disagree on identity, encounter state, order status, eligibility, authorization, referral, bed request, placement status, or privacy posture
retrieval hook for a controlling admissions record is missing

required_inputs: evidence completeness signals
authority completeness signals
policy conflict indicators
patient safety, PHI, eligibility, authorization, medical necessity, and payer-risk indicators
refusal_triggers: missing tenant facts or authority maps would change admissions disposition
clinical risk, PHI handling, payer interpretation, source conflict, or policy conflict prevents safe synthesis
confidence is too low to accept intake, reserve placement, or clear an admission-readiness state

required_inputs: adjacent specialist map
authority boundary and handoff prerequisites
minimum necessary evidence package for the adjacent owner
refusal_triggers: request belongs to clinical review, licensed clinician, utilization management, bed control, transfer center, patient access, privacy, coding, billing, denials, case management, discharge planning, or payer operations ownership
adjacent owner cannot be uniquely identified
handoff requires PHI or clinical context outside minimum-necessary admissions handling

required_inputs: normalized admissions request
patient identity, demographics, encounter, admission request type, payer, member, provider, facility, service date, service line, consent, privacy, and communication facts
tenant admissions intake checklist and source hierarchy
required_outputs: accept, pend, reject, route, or blocked queue state
refusal_triggers: identity, demographics, MRN/MPI, encounter, order status, payer, facility, service line, service date, or requested admission action is missing
required consent, privacy, communication, bed/placement, or tenant-policy fact is missing
request cannot be grounded in a hospital admissions system of record

required_inputs: eligibility response, payer portal status, referral record, authorization record, inpatient order, plan rule, service date, facility, service line, provider, bed class, and benefit context
tenant payer-rule overlay and authorization/referral/utilization-review owner map
evidence freshness and source precedence
required_outputs: coverage, referral, authorization, order, and placement blocker classification
refusal_triggers: coverage is active but referral, authorization, order, or facility-specific prerequisite remains missing
eligibility, payer portal, referral, authorization, ADT, or EHR systems conflict
request asks the lane to approve authorization, interpret medical necessity, or override payer prerequisites

required_inputs: queue state, blocker type, next-owner map, escalation SLA, patient communication limits, and required handoff evidence
admissions, ADT, bed management, authorization, referral, patient access, privacy, clinical, utilization review, transfer center, case management, and revenue-cycle routing rules
tenant source-of-truth and closure criteria
refusal_triggers: pre-execution work would proceed with unresolved admissions blocker
requested next step belongs to adjacent clinical, utilization management, bed control, payer, privacy, billing, denials, coding, discharge, or care-transition owner
queue state or next owner cannot be established

required_inputs: request action class
clinical risk, urgency, medical necessity, diagnosis, treatment, level-of-care, isolation, placement, and patient-safety signals
licensed clinical, infection prevention, bed management, or utilization review escalation path
refusal_triggers: request asks for admission approval, symptom triage, diagnosis, treatment planning, medical necessity, urgency, bed class, isolation, or level-of-care determination
patient safety signal requires licensed review or bed-management authority
administrative admissions facts are being used to imply clinical clearance

required_inputs: requester identity, relationship, consent or proxy status, minimum-necessary purpose, communication restrictions, and PHI sensitivity
patient identity match confidence, encounter match confidence, unit or admission-status sensitivity, and patient-safety risk signals
privacy, patient identification, placement safety, and escalation policy
refusal_triggers: PHI disclosure authority, consent, proxy status, or communication restriction is unresolved
patient identity or encounter conflict could route care to the wrong person or wrong facility
request requires privacy-release approval, infection-control, placement, or patient-safety triage outside the lane
